task.title: Add local-model compatibility notes for review-only tasks.

context.problem_statement: The stack has local/private review policy, read-only reviewer policy, and runtime compatibility guidance, but it does not yet say how to decide whether a local model is appropriate for review-only tasks. Operators need privacy-oriented guidance without over-claiming local model capability or confusing community runtimes with supported source adapters.
background: AGW-049 added local/private review policy under workflows/prompts/policies/local-private-review.md and generated Codex copies under .codex/prompts/policies/.
AGW-040 added read-only reviewer sandbox policy, which should remain the default for review-only local-model tasks.
AGW-043 added docs/runtime-compatibility.md with current, planned, manual-only, and unsupported/unknown runtime surfaces.
AGW-048 added optional receipt effort metrics for token/cost and human burden; AGW-051 should reuse those receipt ideas rather than adding telemetry.

The backlog source examples for AGW-051 are Open Codex, Triplecheck, and Goose; any current runtime-specific claims must be checked against primary runtime docs or explicitly labeled as community/manual-only evidence.

Search at packet creation found current official/local-model documentation for Continue with Ollama, Aider with Ollama, and Goose/Ollama provider setup, plus the OpenAI Codex local-environments page. Treat Open Codex forks as community runtimes, not OpenAI Codex support.
make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 at packet creation reported active_task_count 0, no hazards, no stale tasks, and no unknown-scope tasks.
make agent-token-budget at packet creation reported 105 files and 86870 estimated tokens, result passed.

make kit-status at packet creation reported installed repo-contract-kit 0.4.41, target repo version 0.2.23, runtime adapters none, and 5 modified managed files relative to installed kit metadata; this is known source self-dogfood drift, not git dirt.
non_goals: Do not install, configure, or benchmark local models, Ollama, Goose, Continue, Aider, Open Codex, or any other runtime.
Do not add generated runtime adapters or repo-contract-kit target install/update behavior.
Do not claim that local models are generally better, safer, private, or sufficient for all review tasks.
Do not recommend a specific model name, size, provider, or hardware tier as a universal default.
Do not add hosted telemetry, cloud calls, model download automation, or runtime config files.
Do not change review personas, finding schemas, or receipt schemas unless a small docs-only reference is strictly required.
Do not turn review-only local-model guidance into write-capable implementation-agent guidance.

dirty_or_stale_state: make kit-status reports 5 modified kit-managed files relative to installed repo-contract-kit metadata; git status is clean and this source prompt/docs task does not refresh installed kit files.
finalizer_receipt_paths: source main commit db9a064d58843aaca94411aa56ed51d830751867 completed AGW-048 and origin/main matched it
make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 reported active_task_count 0 after AGW-048

closeout_required_before_start.reason: AGW-048 is implemented, validated, pushed, and source-closed; local main matches origin/main; git status is clean; and task-status reports no active or stale task state.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with one AGW-051 source guidance worker; refresh git status and task-status before edits, verify current runtime/local-model claims against primary docs, and stop if implementation needs runtime installs, generated adapters, repo-contract-kit behavior, or benchmark claims.
evidence_paths: make agent-next selected AGW-051 with dirty false

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the source of prompt, policy, runtime-compatibility, and harness-quality guidance for local agentic review and implementation.

area_contracts.purpose: Canonical policy for private/local review data-boundary and provider notes.
Review-only mutation boundary that local-model reviews must preserve.
Current/planned/manual-only runtime support matrix and privacy/locality guidance.
Canonical review entrypoint that applies policy prompts to review-only work.
Human-facing workflow entrypoint where local-model guidance should be discoverable by operators.

stop_conditions: Stop if the worker cannot verify a runtime-specific local-model claim from primary docs or clearly mark it as community/manual-only.
Stop if guidance would require installing a model, changing runtime config, adding adapters, or mutating repo-contract-kit.
Stop if docs imply local models are automatically private when endpoints may be remote, logged, or provider-hosted.
Stop if docs imply local models are adequate for security, compliance, migration, or public API review without human/cloud-capable escalation.
Stop if generated Codex adapters drift from canonical prompt sources.

protected_files: /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/workflows/manifest.json unless prompt adapter export proves a manifest typo
/Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/.agent-workflows/ runtime state except reading deterministic reports
runtime configuration files such as .continue/, .goosehints, .gooseignore, .aider.conf.yml, .env, Ollama config, or model caches
model downloads, benchmark output, hosted telemetry, provider credentials, account state, or CI upload configuration
unrelated backlog rows and unrelated dirty work
inspect_first: Current primary docs for local-model runtime support if making tool-specific claims, including Continue/Ollama docs, Aider/Ollama docs, Goose provider/Ollama docs, and OpenAI Codex docs for Codex-specific boundaries
expected_outputs: Local/private review policy includes a local-model suitability section for review-only work: useful for low-risk triage, summarization, duplicate finding checks, documentation/readme scans, and privacy-sensitive first passes; weak for security-critical, migration, compliance, public API, complex architecture, or high-blast-radius review unless validated and human-escalated.

Policy guidance distinguishes local-only, self-hosted, remote OpenAI-compatible endpoints, and hosted provider use; it tells agents to record the boundary in receipts instead of assuming local privacy.

Docs explain capability expectations for local models: context limits, tool-calling reliability, structured-output reliability, repo scale, latency, hardware variability, stale model knowledge, and higher false-positive/false-negative risk.

Runtime compatibility docs add local-model notes for review-only tasks without claiming generated adapter support. Current primary-doc-supported surfaces should be cited with access dates; community forks such as Open Codex must be separated from OpenAI Codex support.

Review prompt entrypoints tell reviewers to apply local-model compatibility notes only for review-only tasks, preserve read-only sandbox rules, and escalate when evidence quality or model capability is insufficient.
Docs link the local-model notes from README or using-the-prompt-kit so operators can find them before choosing a model/provider.
Generated Codex prompt adapters mirror canonical prompt policy changes.
Version/changelog and source backlog rows close AGW-051 only after validation.

acceptance_criteria.description: Local/private review policy documents when local models are appropriate for review-only tasks and when to escalate to a stronger/human-reviewed path.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect workflows/prompts/policies/local-private-review.md and generated .codex copy after adapter export.

acceptance_criteria.description: Runtime compatibility docs include local-model notes that distinguish primary-doc-supported local runtimes from manual-only/community/unsupported surfaces without adding generated adapter claims.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect docs/runtime-compatibility.md and docs/runtime-adapters.md if changed; confirm source URLs and access dates for current runtime claims.

acceptance_criteria.description: Guidance tells agents to record actual data boundaries, provider/model expectations, capability caveats, and escalation decisions in receipts or handoff notes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect local/private policy, review entrypoint, README, and docs/using-the-prompt-kit.md if changed.

acceptance_criteria.description: The implementation does not install models, edit runtime config, add repo-contract-kit behavior, add telemetry, or claim local-model productivity/security guarantees.
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff --name-only compared with allowed/protected scope.

acceptance_criteria.description: Generated Codex adapters are refreshed if canonical prompts or policies changed.
Version/changelog and source backlog rows close AGW-051 only after validation passes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: make version-check, make backlog-check, and make backlog-split-check.

pass; pre-existing unchanged instruction-hygiene warnings may be reported but must be identified

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the source checkout is clean, only expected files remain dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This is a prompt/docs guidance change. Update README/docs and changelog so operators can find the local-model review-only caveats.

known_risks: Runtime/model capability claims can drift quickly; use primary sources and access dates or keep language capability-based.
Local model guidance can be misread as a privacy guarantee even when a local-looking endpoint is remote or logs prompts.
Capability caveats can become vague; keep escalation rules concrete enough for agents to act on.
Generated prompt adapters can drift if canonical policy files are edited without export/check.
stop_conditions: A required source is unavailable and the claim would be current/runtime-specific.
The change would need runtime installs, model downloads, provider credentials, or target-repo config files.
The change would promote local models for high-risk review without human or stronger-model escalation.
Validation or prompt adapter sync fails.

approval.notes: Approved as part of the workflow-stack backlog execution request; keep implementation bounded to source guidance and docs.

overlap_warnings: Refresh git status and task-status before worker edits.
Do not edit repo-contract-kit or runtime configuration files.
Treat installed kit managed-file drift as known self-dogfood state unless it becomes actual git dirt or affects validation.

handoff.next_packet_hint: AGW-055 remains the next agent-workflow-kit backlog item after AGW-051 unless repo-contract-kit P2 items are prioritized.
